# Chapter 1: Modelling dynamic systems  (~100 pp)
- 1.1 What a system is, what a model is (12) · 1.2 Lumped vs distributed (12) · 1.3 Linear vs nonlinear (12) · 1.4 Time-invariant vs time-varying (10) · 1.5 Block diagrams and signal-flow graphs (15) · 1.6 System identification introduced (15) · 1.7 Worked examples across mechanical, thermal, electrical, biological, social systems (15) · 1.8 Failure section: model misspecification at the foundation (9)

**Archetype**: balance, scaling. **Project**: identify a real dynamic system from input-output data; defend the model class. **Exercises**: 35.
